info.name: Auth – Google OAuth (GET /api/v1/oauth/google + callback + DELETE /api/v1/oauth/google)
info.description:
E2E tests for Google OAuth endpoints.

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-oauth-google):

  Invocation 1 (single Newman run — JWT token must survive across folders):
    1. setup             — register gmail_test_email, fetch verification OTP from Gmail, verify email, login
    2. initiate          — GET /oauth/google → 302 to Google; assert PKCE params; capture state UUID
    3. callback-guards   — guard cases that do not require a real Google code (state/code missing or unknown)
    4. unlink-failures   — auth failures + not_found for DELETE /oauth/google
    5. rate-limiting-unl — register +goauthr alias, login, burst=5 exhaustion → 429 (Retry-After: 180)

  Invocation 2 (after Redis flush — fresh goauth:init:ip: bucket):
    6. rate-limiting-init — 20 warmups GET /oauth/google from 127.0.0.1 → 21st → 429 (Retry-After: 15)

  Invocation 3 (after Redis flush — fresh goauth:cb:ip: bucket):
    7. rate-limiting-cb   — 20 warmups GET /oauth/google/callback from 127.0.0.1 → 21st → 429 (Retry-After: 15)

NOT covered here and why:
  - Full callback happy path (login / register / email-match / link modes): Requires a real Google-issued
    authorization code obtainable only through a browser OAuth flow. Covered by service_test.go T-12 to T-35
    and store_test.go T-36 to T-38.
  - Unlink happy path (T-39): Requires a user with a linked Google identity created via the real callback.
    Covered by service_test.go T-39 and store_test.go T-51.
  - last_auth_method guard (T-42, T-43): Requires a Google-only user (no password_hash). Covered by
    service_test.go T-42 and handler_test.go T-43.
  - oauth_access_token and refresh_token cookie flags (T-30, T-31, T-32): Covered by handler_test.go.
  - Audit row content and DB-level metadata: Covered by store_test.go.
  - context.WithoutCancel timing invariants (T-33, T-34, T-48): Covered by service_test.go.
  - Individual field-level service errors: Covered by service_test.go.
  - DB-level error injection via QuerierProxy: Covered by store_test.go.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.
// rate-limiting-init and rate-limiting-cb override _xff to 127.0.0.1 in their
// folder prerequest so all warmup requests hit the same IP bucket.

item.name: setup
item.description:
Registers gmail_test_email, fetches the verification OTP from Gmail, verifies the email, and logs in. Stores goauth_access_token for use by later folders.

This user is registered with a password and therefore has no linked Google identity. The unlink-failures folder uses goauth_access_token to confirm the not_found path (no Google identity linked for this user).

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + goauthEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching verification email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode verification email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in verification email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Verification OTP stored in goauth_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] goauth_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in register step did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
console.log('[INFO] Access token stored in goauth_access_token.');

item.name: initiate
item.description:
Verifies that GET /api/v1/oauth/google returns a 302 redirect to Google with correct PKCE and state parameters.

All requests must NOT follow redirects (protocolProfileBehavior.followRedirects=false). Without this, Newman follows the 302 to accounts.google.com and assertions are made against the wrong response.

The second request (authenticated) captures the state UUID from the Location header into goauth_state_from_initiate for use in the callback-guards folder.

exec: // HandleInitiate returns 302 with Location pointing to accounts.google.com.
// The PKCE code_challenge and state UUID are included as query params.
// This request has no Authorization header — anonymous users can initiate OAuth.
// An authenticated user initiating OAuth (link mode).
// HandleInitiate encodes the user ID in the KV state so the callback can link the account.
// The response is still a 302 to accounts.google.com — same as unauthenticated.
// Store it in goauth_state_from_initiate for the callback-guards folder.
console.warn('[WARN] Could not extract state from Location header.');

item.name: callback-guards
item.description:
Verifies every pre-service guard in HandleCallback that can be triggered without a real Google authorization code.

All requests must NOT follow redirects (protocolProfileBehavior.followRedirects=false). The callback never returns a JSON body — every outcome is a 302 redirect. Error codes appear as ?error=<code> in the Location header.

The final request (valid KV state, missing code) uses goauth_state_from_initiate captured by the initiate folder. This state was created by a real initiate call, so it exists in KV. The callback deletes it on first use, which also implicitly verifies the single-use property.

exec: // When Google returns ?error=access_denied the handler redirects to OAuthErrorURL
// with ?error=oauth_cancelled immediately, before any KV or token exchange.
// No state param at all — the handler cannot find a KV entry.
// Guard: state is required → redirect to OAuthErrorURL with ?error=invalid_state.
// state param is present but refers to a UUID that was never stored in KV.
// KV Get miss → redirect to OAuthErrorURL with ?error=invalid_state.
console.warn('[WARN] goauth_state_from_initiate is empty — the initiate folder\'s second request did not capture it.');
console.warn('        This test will still run but may not exercise the KV-hit guard.');
// state is present and exists in KV (created by the initiate folder), but code is missing.
// Guard: code is required → redirect with ?error=invalid_state.
// This request also implicitly verifies the single-use property: the callback deletes
// the KV entry on first use, so a second request with the same state would also get
// invalid_state (KV miss on the second attempt).
pm.test('Location contains error=invalid_state (valid KV state, missing code)', () => {

item.name: unlink-failures
item.description:
Verifies that DELETE /api/v1/oauth/google correctly handles auth failures and the not_found domain error.

Unlike initiate and callback, unlink returns JSON. No followRedirects override is needed.

The third request uses goauth_access_token (setup user). The setup user was registered via email+password and has no linked Google identity, so ErrIdentityNotFound is returned and the handler responds 404.

NOTE: This folder consumes 1 slot of the goauth:unl:usr: bucket for the setup user's bucket.
The rate-limiting-unl folder uses a dedicated +goauthr alias user (goauth_rl_access_token) to guarantee a fresh burst=5 bucket.

exec: // No Authorization header — the JWTAuth middleware must reject this before the
// handler body or rate limiter runs.
// A JWT with the last character of the signature replaced by 'X'.
// The JWTAuth middleware validates the access token and must reject this with 401.

item.name: DELETE /oauth/google (valid JWT, no Google identity linked) → 404 not_found

exec: // This request uses goauth_access_token. The setup user has no linked Google
// identity, so ErrIdentityNotFound is returned and the handler responds 404.
// This also confirms the JWT auth → rate limiter → handler wiring is correct end-to-end.
// NOTE: This consumes 1 slot of the goauth:unl:usr: bucket for the setup user.
// The rate-limiting-unl folder uses a dedicated alias user (goauth_rl_access_token)
// to guarantee a fresh burst=5 bucket.

item.name: rate-limiting-unl
item.description:
Verifies the goauth:unl:usr: user rate limiter for DELETE /oauth/google (burst=5, rate=5/15 min, Retry-After=180 s).

Why a dedicated user (goauth_rl_access_token):
  The goauth:unl:usr: limiter is user-keyed. The unlink-failures folder consumed 1 slot from
  goauth_access_token's bucket. To guarantee a fresh burst=5 bucket, this folder registers and logs
  in as a dedicated gmail_test_email+goauthr alias user. Gmail delivers +goauthr mail to the same inbox;
  the OTP fetch targets the alias with a to: filter.

Sequence:
  rl-setup 1-3: register +goauthr alias → verify email (Gmail OTP) → login (store goauth_rl_access_token)
  warmup 1-5:   not 429 (slots 1-5 consumed; each returns 404 not_found since the RL user has no
                Google identity, but the rate limiter fires first so slots are consumed regardless)
  6th request → 429 too_many_requests (bucket exhausted)

Retry-After = ceil(1 / (5/900)) = ceil(180) = 180 seconds.

exec: // Derive the +goauthr Gmail alias from gmail_test_email.
// Gmail delivers user+goauthr@gmail.com to the same inbox as user@gmail.com.
// Fetch the verification OTP for the +goauthr alias specifically.
// Using to: filter so we get the right email when both users registered in the same hour.
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for +goauthr verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + rlEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +goauthr verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +goauthr email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +goauthr email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] +goauthr OTP stored in goauth_rl_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] goauth_rl_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in rl-setup 1/3 did not complete.');
console.log('[INFO] RL access token stored in goauth_rl_access_token.');
// Uses goauth_rl_access_token (fresh bucket — no prior unlink calls for this user).
// Returns 404 not_found (RL user has no linked Google identity), but the rate
// limiter fires first so the slot is still consumed.

item.name: warmup 5/5 — consume slot (→ not 429, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (goauth:unl:usr: slot 5 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// burst=5 is exhausted. The rate limiter fires before the handler.

item.name: rate-limiting-init
item.description:
Verifies the goauth:init:ip: IP rate limiter for GET /oauth/google (burst=20, rate=20/5 min, Retry-After=15 s).

All requests pin _xff to 127.0.0.1 (folder prerequest override) so all warmup hits land on the same IP bucket.
All requests must NOT follow redirects — GET /oauth/google returns 302 when not rate-limited.

Retry-After = ceil(1 / (20/300)) = ceil(15) = 15 seconds.

exec: // Override _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all requests in this folder hit the same IP bucket.
// The collection prerequest has already run and set a 10.x.x.x value; we overwrite it here.
// goauth:init:ip: is IP-keyed. All warmup requests come from 127.0.0.1
// (overridden in folder prerequest). The 302 response from /oauth/google
// confirms the rate limiter passed this request through. Slot 1 consumed.

item.name: warmup 20/20 — consume slot (→ not 429, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (goauth:init:ip: slot 20 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// burst=20 is exhausted. The rate limiter fires before the handler.

item.name: rate-limiting-cb
item.description:
Verifies the goauth:cb:ip: IP rate limiter for GET /oauth/google/callback (burst=20, rate=20/5 min, Retry-After=15 s).

All requests pin _xff to 127.0.0.1 (folder prerequest override) so all warmup hits land on the same IP bucket.
All requests must NOT follow redirects — the callback always returns a 302.

Each warmup request uses ?error=test which does not match access_denied, so the handler proceeds to check state, finds none, and redirects with error=invalid_state. The rate limiter fires before the handler body, so the slot is consumed regardless of the error code produced.

Retry-After = ceil(1 / (20/300)) = ceil(15) = 15 seconds.

exec: // Override _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all requests in this folder hit the same IP bucket.
// goauth:cb:ip: is IP-keyed. All warmup requests come from 127.0.0.1.
// Any request to /callback returns 302 (e.g. ?error=test → oauth_cancelled or invalid_state).
// The rate limiter fires before the handler, so the slot is consumed regardless. Slot 1 consumed.

item.name: warmup 20/20 — consume slot (→ not 429, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (goauth:cb:ip: slot 20 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// burst=20 is exhausted. The rate limiter fires before the handler.

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique 10.x.x.x IP by the collection prerequest. Overridden to 127.0.0.1 by rate-limiting-init and rate-limiting-cb folder prerequest.
Set from gmail_test_email in setup/register prerequest.
Setup user password (P@ssw0rd1!). Set in setup/register prerequest.
Verification OTP for goauth_email fetched from Gmail by setup/register test script.
Access token for goauth_email from setup/login. Used by initiate (authenticated) and unlink-failures folders.
State UUID captured from the Location header of the authenticated GET /oauth/google request in the initiate folder. Used by the last request in callback-guards.
gmail_test_email+goauthr alias. Derived and set in rate-limiting-unl rl-setup 1/3 prerequest. Fresh goauth:unl:usr: bucket — not used by any other folder.
Password for goauth_rl_email (RlGoauthP@ss1!). Set in rate-limiting-unl rl-setup 1/3 prerequest.
Verification OTP for goauth_rl_email fetched from Gmail by rl-setup 1/3 test script (to: filter targets the +goauthr alias).
Access token for goauth_rl_email from rl-setup 3/3. Used exclusively by rate-limiting-unl warmups and the 6th-request trigger. Guarantees a fresh burst=5 goauth:unl:usr: bucket.
